name: invest-04-queue-tech-choice
initialPrompt: We need to add async job processing to our platform — things like sending emails, resizing images, and running nightly reports. Right now everything is synchronous and it's blocking our web workers. I need a plan to: evaluate and pick a queue technology (we're considering Redis-backed BullMQ, RabbitMQ, and SQS), design the job schema and retry/dead-letter strategy, and migrate the first two existing synchronous flows to use the queue.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Async job queue: technology selection and initial migration

blocks.text: Offload blocking work from web workers to a reliable async job queue
successCriteria: web worker p99 response time drops by 40%
zero silent job loss — all failures land in DLQ

blocks.question: Which queue technology should we adopt — BullMQ, RabbitMQ, or SQS?

blocks.title: Spike: benchmark all three queue options under 10k jobs/min load
acceptance: throughput, latency, and ops burden documented

blocks.title: Design job envelope schema: type, payload, retryPolicy, idempotencyKey
Migrate synchronous email dispatch to queue-backed worker
acceptance: email dispatch non-blocking in web process
delivery confirmed via webhook in staging

blocks.description: BullMQ depends on Redis availability; Redis outage stops all job processing
blocks.mitigation: Run Redis in sentinel or cluster mode; implement circuit breaker

blocks.question: Who gets paged when a job lands in the dead-letter queue — on-call engineer or the owning team?
